title: stewartia villosa var. grandifolia
intro: Stewartia villosa var. grandifolia, also known as the large-leaf stewartia, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, belonging to the Theaceae family of plants. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Stewartia villosa var. grandifolia include its potential to treat fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea or decoction, and its bark and leaves can also be used to make infusions, tinctures, and extracts for medicinal purposes.
